# Tensorgami

> Public mathematical workbench by Henry Shin for expositions, proof notes,
> problem sets, Lean formalizations, and explicit proof mechanisms.

## Start here

- [Verification](https://tensorgami.com/verification.html): selected verified artifacts, theorem names, Lean source, and CI checks.
- [Mechanism Map](https://tensorgami.com/mechanisms.html): recurring proof patterns across the archive.
- [About](https://tensorgami.com/about.html): maintainer, contact, and external links.

## Best artifacts

- [IBM Ponder This July 2010](https://tensorgami.com/notes/ibm-ponder-this-2010.html): trace and companion-matrix solution to the July 2010 IBM Ponder This challenge; Lean theorem `IBMPonderThisFinal.ibm_ponder_this_main`.
- [Divisibility of Separated Polynomials](https://tensorgami.com/notes/separated-polynomial-divisibility.html): separated polynomial divisibility criterion; Lean theorem `SeparatedPolynomialDifferences.sep_dvd_iff_exists_common_outer`.
- [A Galois-Theoretic and Frobenius-Descent Viewpoint on a Polynomial Composition Law](https://tensorgami.com/notes/galois-frobenius-composition-law.html): composition law through generic fibers, Galois descent, and Frobenius reduction; Lean theorem family `SeparatedComposition.separated_composition_criterion_iff`.
- [Extracting the First Prime Greater than m](https://tensorgami.com/notes/extracting-first-prime.html): finite extraction formula for the least prime above `m`; Lean theorem `ExtractingFirstPrime.extractedPrime_is_smallest_prime_gt`.
- [A Note on Disjoint Decompositions of [0,1) into Closed Sets](https://tensorgami.com/notes/disjoint-decompositions.html): boundary obstruction and Baire-category proof; Lean theorem `no_pairwiseDisjoint_closed_iUnion_eq_Ico`.

## Verification

- [Lean formalizations](https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/tree/main/formalizations)
- [GitHub Actions Lean check](https://github.com/Tensorgami/tensorgami.github.io/actions/workflows/verify-lean.yml)
- [Machine-readable portfolio](https://tensorgami.com/portfolio.json)
